news.title: Government identifying new site for Unity Mall: Goa tourism minister
news.para: Goa's Tourism Minister Rohan Khaunte announced the state government is identifying a new location for the Unity Mall. The previous Chimbel site was abandoned considering local community emotions. The minister stated the opposition was political. Rs 7.1 crore has been disbursed for the project. A consultant's claim is under review. The new site will be a cabinet decision.

news.title: MHADA targeting 20,000 affordable homes through cluster redevelopment in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai is shifting to large-scale cluster redevelopment projects. This strategy aims to unlock significant land for housing and boost affordable housing supply. MHADA is prioritizing redevelopment of aging housing stock and expanding affordable housing for lower income groups. Cluster projects are expected to generate substantial housing units and premiums over the coming years.

news.title: Hubballi Dharwad civic body may soon get nod for 64,000 LED streetlights
news.para: Hubballi-Dharwad's long-awaited LED streetlight project is set to commence within weeks. After a delay, the selected agency has submitted its bank surety, clearing the path for the Rs 93.6-crore initiative. This project will replace conventional lights with 64,000 LED lamps, promising energy savings, improved safety, and a centralized monitoring system for enhanced efficiency.

news.title: Pune civic body allocates ₹954 crore for development of merged villages
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation's draft budget allocates Rs954 crore for newly merged areas, prioritizing infrastructure development. The budget, driven by projected revenue from building permissions and property taxes, aims for inclusive growth and uniform development across the city. Funds are earmarked for roads, drainage, and a 24x7 water supply project.

news.title: Registration department mulls clearing tenant agreements from any sub-registrar office in Pune
news.para: Pune's property registration department is planning to let any sub-registrar office in the district clear tenant agreements online. This move aims to reduce delays and speed up approvals. The system is already working in Mumbai and Thane. Officials are addressing software issues in Pune.

news.title: Nashik civic body collects ₹272 crore property tax in FY26 so far
news.para: Nashik Municipal Corporation achieved a historic Rs 272 crore in property tax collection for the financial year 2025-26. This record collection was boosted by a 15-day amnesty scheme for defaulters, generating Rs 46 crore. Incentives for prompt taxpayers and the addition of 30,000 new properties to the tax net also contributed significantly to the civic body's revenue.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh to launch OTS scheme for property defaulters
news.para: Uttar Pradesh housing development authorities will soon launch a one-time settlement scheme. This initiative aims to help defaulters clear their outstanding dues. The scheme will cover residential, commercial, and other property types. It offers a chance to settle pending payments with potential waivers on charges and penalties. Simple interest will be applied. The scheme will be available for three months.

news.title: ED charges five with laundering ₹36.66 crore from illegal sand mining in Patna
news.para: In a significant legal development, the Enforcement Directorate has leveled charges against Arun Yadav, Kiran Devi, Rajesh Kumar Ranjan, Dipu Kumar, and their corporation for alleged money laundering activities in Patna. This case revolves around illicit sand mining operations and associated corruption, resulting in the emergence of over Rs 36 crore in unexplained assets.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad: PCMC to take strict action on RMC plants violating pollution norms, may seal units
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Municipal Corporation is cracking down on Ready Mix Concrete plants violating pollution norms. With the city's air quality in the moderate range, civic officials are conducting inspections and warning that non-compliant units face closure and sealing. This move follows stricter guidelines from the Maharashtra Pollution Control Board, aiming to curb dust and air pollution affecting residents.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government allocates ₹425 crore for townships in eight cities
news.para: Uttar Pradesh government has allocated Rs 425 crore for developing new townships in eight key cities, including Bareilly and Varanasi. This seed capital, under the Chief Minister New Township Scheme, aims to facilitate planned urban growth and improve housing for a growing population. The government will fund up to 50% of land acquisition costs.

news.title: Maharashtra: New stamp duty penalty effective from January 1 this year
news.para: A significant increase in the penalty for property documents executed without sufficient stamp duty is now in effect from January 1, 2026. The revised penalty, raised to Rs1 lakh from Rs5,000, aims to curb deliberate underpayment of stamp duty. This amendment strengthens action against those evading required stamp duty on property transactions.

news.title: Chandigarh administration proposes structured penalties, clearer definitions of property categories
news.para: Chandigarh is set to overhaul building violation and property misuse regulations by amending the Capital of Punjab (Development and Regulation) Act, 1952. New definitions for property categories, structured penalties capped at 20% of property value, and enhanced administrative powers are being introduced.  Appeals will now be streamlined to the chief administrator within 30 days.

news.title: Paint makers fear thinner profits on crude oil spike
news.para: Domestic paint makers face renewed profitability challenges as rising crude oil prices, driven by West Asian conflict, increase raw material costs. Derivatives of crude oil constitute a significant portion of input expenses, threatening to erode margins. Companies anticipate an impact in the next quarter as higher costs filter through their structure.

news.title: Mumbai police arrest Kolhapur developer in ₹55 crore Versova scam
news.para: Mumbai police arrested a Kolhapur developer for a Rs 55 crore fraud. Amrut Uttam Mahendrakar allegedly helped forge documents to mortgage and sell flats belonging to society members. These flats were part of a redevelopment project in Versova. The developer secured bank loans using these fraudulent transactions. This is the second arrest in the case.

news.title: Haryana RERA rejects pleas of multiple buyers, cites lack of jurisdiction
news.para: Haryana Rera dismissed multiple homebuyer complaints, citing jurisdictional issues and prior relief. One case involved a maintenance security deposit dispute outside Rera's purview. Other dismissals were for additional compensation claims on delayed possession, as allottees had already received statutory interest for the delay.

news.title: Cabinet clears revised cost of ₹3,630.77 crore for developing road connectivity to Jewar airport
news.para: The Union Cabinet has approved a revised cost of over Rs 3,600 crore for road connectivity to the upcoming Jewar International Airport. This project will create a 31.42-kilometer corridor. It will offer direct, high-speed access from South Delhi, Faridabad, and Gurugram. The corridor will also intersect major expressways and freight corridors.

news.title: Mysuru civic body to adopt LiDAR tech to increase property tax collection
news.para: Mysuru City Corporation is adopting LiDAR technology to boost property tax collection. This advanced mapping system accurately identifies property boundaries, structures, and land use changes, aiming to increase revenue by 30% to Rs 300 crore. The technology, successfully used in other cities, will help bring unauthorized constructions under the tax net.

news.title: IFC, GBPN and Smarter Dharma collaborate on green materials platform to boost sustainable construction in India
news.para: India is experiencing a transformative shift in its urban development, with Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities emerging as hubs of opportunity. A new innovative platform, MGEM, is stepping up to support this growth by enhancing the adoption of green building practices. It delivers trusted green construction resources while facilitating access to financing aimed at sustainable development.
